items.headline: Albemarle biggest beneficiary of expanding lithium demand - Citi
items.snippet: Citi: Albemarle will become a key beneficiary of the structural expansion in lithium demand based on its excellent asset portfolio. Global ESS production forecast 950 GWh this year up 35% YoY.

items.headline: Does Albemarle Premium Valuation Justify Buying the Stock Now?
items.snippet: Higher lithium prices driven by strong demand from EVs and energy storage systems. Operating cash flow was around $1.3 billion in 2025 up roughly 86%. ALB had liquidity of around $2.7 billion.

items.headline: Albemarle vs. Rio Tinto: Which Lithium Stock Holds More Promise?
items.snippet: ALB expects lithium demand to witness a CAGR of 10-20% from 2025 to 2030. Stationary storage is expected to be a significant driver for lithium demand along with EVs. Global ESS production rising 117% year over year.

items.headline: Albemarle Likely to Continue Growing Volumes Over the Coming Years, RBC Says
items.snippet: Albemarle (ALB) is likely to continue growing volumes over the coming years from mostly brownfield at Wodgina, Atacama productivity, and opportunities at its Kings Mountain and Antofalla hubs. Lithium supply is expected to remain tight through 2027. RBC boosted price target to $2
